Guideline: Polite/formal register (desu/masu form). Standard keigo throughout.
Professional and respectful. Polite forms throughout.
Japanese is inherently gender-neutral in most constructions.
N/A for Japanese characters. English loanwords and acronyms in half-width Latin characters.
Japanese corner brackets used for feature/widget names consistently.
Full-width colons for labels. Standard Japanese periods and commas.
{{variable}} placeholders preserved. Japanese counting patterns. MB for megabytes.
Kanji compound nouns for domain terms. Katakana for transliterated English terms.

Extensive katakana transliteration: widget, check-in, feedback, template, coaching, custom field, entity, status. Kept in English: Analytics Hub, Data Foundation, Data Modeler, PII, API, CSV, SFTP, True, False.
Noun form or polite command for buttons. For longer instructions: ...shite kudasai (please...).
Enable: [X] feature wo yuukoka. Confirmations: ...shitemo yoroshii desu ka? Success: ...seijou ni...saremashita. Empty states: Mada...ga arimasen.

Check-in=chekkuin; Review=rebyuu; Goal=mokuhyou; Worker=shain/juugyouin; Manager=maneejaa; Compensation=houshuu; Behavior=koudou; Career Development=kyaria kaihatsu; Settings=settei; Library=raiburari
